is_p_falciparum.evidence: Study of Plasmodium falciparum 3D7 strain gene expression.

reports_target_markers.evidence: PfMDR1 is discussed as a differentially expressed gene (upregulated ~5.5-fold by SAGE), but no codon 86 (or any resistance codon) genotyping is reported. This is a gene expression study, not a marker frequency study.

reports_extractable_frequencies.evidence: Only transcript fold-changes and tag counts are given; no allele/mutation frequencies (numerator/denominator) at any codon.

sub_country_location.evidence: In vitro laboratory culture of 3D7 strain; no field sampling location at any level.

temporal_resolution_within_3y.evidence: No field collection window; laboratory cultured parasites only.

african_field_samples.evidence: 3D7 is a laboratory reference strain cultured in vitro; no African field/clinical isolates.

is_primary_study.evidence: Primary empirical SAGE and RT-PCR/Northern experiments on cultured parasites.

duplicate_risk.evidence: Laboratory culture experiment; no shared field cohort.

supplement.evidence: No supplementary data would provide resistance-marker frequencies; the study is a transcriptomic experiment on a lab strain.

assessment.summary: This is a laboratory transcriptomics study using SAGE to profile chloroquine-induced gene expression changes in the cultured P. falciparum 3D7 reference strain. Although PfMDR1 is mentioned as upregulated, the paper reports only transcript fold-changes, not drug-resistance codon genotype frequencies. It uses a lab strain, not African field isolates, and has no sub-national or temporal field-sampling data.
exclusion_reasons: No drug-resistance codon markers genotyped (only gene expression of PfMDR1)
No sub-country field location (in vitro 3D7 lab strain)
Uses laboratory reference strain, not African field/clinical isolates
